response: Programmers favor interfaces over abstract classes because they address a fundamental lack of foresight regarding future object relationships, allowing systems to evolve without rigid inheritance hierarchies. While abstract classes enforce a strict path of implementation, interfaces enable loose coupling where a file or module can implement multiple unrelated contracts simultaneously. This flexibility prevents the code from becoming a tangled salad of dependencies, ensuring that new behaviors can be added to existing objects without altering their original construction or breaking unrelated parts of the system.
